channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Mixed rubbish outside school, 6th March
item.description:
Mixed rubbish outside school

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Peacock Street, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large pothole in centre of road opposite junction of Brampton Road & Longleigh Lane, 6th March
item.description:
Pothole has increased in size over the past week. Is likely to cause vehicle damage if not repaired ASAP
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brampton Road, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 12 meyers x 1meter broken road, 6th March
item.description:
This is as a result of lorries in and out last year when building the new school turnstiles. The road that is broken is on a bend and the zigzag markings & SCHOOL words that should be visible on the road have now gone. It is dangerous for motorists.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hartford Road, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Long pothole. Been there a tear. You fixed one nearby but left this. Its deep and unavoidable when vehicles come immediately around the corner near no 49 Hartford road., 6th March
item.description:
This pothole has been there about a year and is getting much worse. More cars are coming along Hartford Road due to a new entrance for the students at Beth's so more wear on the road as the young drivers race up to park.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hartford Road, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 2 deep potholes near major facilities, 6th March
item.description:
2 deep potholes near the Bus Garage, Barnehurst Ambulance Station, and the fire station. Please take a look. They get deeper and deeper every day, and I can see that it is a high-traffic area for ambulances and fire trucks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Erith Road, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cones blocking parking on the road, 6th March
item.description:
There is an wilful obstruction of the highway. Cones are placed outside the houses on the road, frequently during late afternoon during weekdays preventing parking. This has been occurring within last few weeks. There is a school within close proximity and presumably it is to prevent parking during those hours.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Denbigh Close, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Several drains are blocked, 6th March
item.description:
Every single time it rains these drains block up. Repeatedly 'repaired but there has to be a problem with these drains they do not work in any wet weather. 3 drains in total from the corner and on to the 2 drains in the carpark. I think there maybe an underlying issue with all 3 drains as it's been continuing for years

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bliss Crescent, Lewisham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A 1 meter width pothole in the middle of the road, 6th March
item.description:
A 1-meter-wide pothole in the middle of the road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Watling Street, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter all over Selby Road, not fixed, 6th March
item.description:
Already reported and received update to say ‘fixed’ which is not true. Litter still all over Selby Road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 48, Selby Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Gas cylinders, 6th March
item.description:
They are a nuisance
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 35, Berens Way, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Aftermath from fight, 6th March
item.description:
There is a lot of blood and a paramedics glove at the foot of the bridge from a fight on Sunday night. Picture is from this morning 6/3. Police have just left it like that and the council still haven’t cleaned it.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Boord Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lamppost not working again 18 Denbridge road, 6th March
item.description:
Lamppost not working outside 18 Denbridge road reported mid December fixed two and half months later on 3rd March worked for two nights now not working yet again
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Denbridge Road, Bickley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: M&S trolley filled with rubish, coffee cups and carboard postal, 6th March
item.description:
Good afternoon, I would like to report an ongoing issue involving abandoned supermarket trolleys and waste being left in front of the Bromley Picturehouse cinema on High Street, Bromley, and in the shared garden area at my property. Over a long period of time I have noticed dozens of supermarket trolleys being left outside the cinema on High Street. This morning I personally returned a Sainsbury’s trolley that had been left there. Later around lunchtime I also found an M&S trolley which I returned to the Piranha shop after noticing it contained litter, rubbish, and a box with the shop’s address on it and the owner’s name, Joe Perry, Piranha Comics, 246 High Street, BR11PQ. When I brought it back and talked to one of the Team members, she acknowledged to be theirs. Mr. Joe Perry occasionally leaves supermarket trolleys in my garden at 246A High Street, Bromley, where he has a storage room. These trolleys are not returned by him, and I am usually the person who ends up taking them back myself. In addition, items removed from his storage room have been left in the shared garden area near the entrance for the past 2–3 years. This waste has been accumulating and is now attracting large pests, which is becoming a significant nuisance and a potential health concern. I would appreciate it if this matter could be investigated and appropriate action taken to prevent further dumping of trolleys and waste in this area. Thank you for your support.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 234, High Street, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog mess all over pavements, 6th March
item.description:
When I take my children to and from school along South Hill Road there is constant dog mess spoiling the pavements on both sides. It is becoming a real problem for everyone walking around the area.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 89, South Hill Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large bag and box blocking pavement, 6th March
item.description:
Large bag and box blocking pavement

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Finsbury Park Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish has been dumped, 6th March
item.description:
Rubbish has been dumped on the grass patch

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Somersham Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large Tree needs reducing, 6th March
item.description:
The tree overhang our property and causes a nuisance. Tree surgeons called round 2 weeks ago but we were out and our car was on the drive. They said they would return but haven’t yet. We would like the tree reduced and branches which overhang our property removed

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 40, Poverest Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mattresses and bed dumped outside ur home and in goose green, 6th March
item.description:
Mattresses and bed frame dumped outside our home and bed frame in goose green

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 61, East Dulwich Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: By the recycling bins at James Stewart House., 6th March
item.description:
Reported mattress yesterday. Now there is a sofa, another mattress and a fridge.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Dyne Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Large - More than a van load (Estates)

item.title: Large pothole, 6th March
item.description:
A large pothole on Chislehurst Road, Bickley, it’s right by the side of speed hump which means everyone hits it. Someone has put a carpet in it to lessen the damage. It’s really serious and needs urgent attention
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Chislehurst Road, Bickley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
